methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Wardstone operates in Space & Aerospace at the seed stage. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Planet Labs, and Firefly Aerospace—a total of 13 reference points across the sector.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median multiples proved inapplicable: while 23 same-sector seed or adjacent-stage companies exist, none yielded three or more datapoints meeting narrow filtering criteria (same stage, adjacent stage, or comparable valuation tier). Public-comp anchoring was also not feasible; the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 45.3x price-to-sales across the six public comps, but Wardstone's revenue remains undisclosed—standard practice for private companies—precluding multiple application.

The analysis cannot support a quantitative valuation range given the absence of applicable comparable-company multiples and missing financial data. Key uncertainties include Wardstone's current revenue run rate, capital efficiency relative to sector peers, and technology differentiation within a capital-intensive sector. A complete valuation would require either (1) disclosed revenue metrics enabling public-comp anchoring, (2) sufficient seed-stage private comparables with recent funding rounds, or (3) alternative methods such as discounted cash flow or venture-capital method analysis using explicit growth and return assumptions. Further diligence on operating metrics is prerequisite to establishing defensible valuation support.
